title: Part 2 – Entraînement 5 : mix de types de questions
instructions: Écoutez chaque question et les trois réponses. Choisissez la réponse la plus appropriée.

items.audioText: Has anyone seen the updated price list? (A) I think Sarah has a copy. (B) The prices went up last quarter. (C) The list is very long.
choices: I think Sarah has a copy.
The prices went up last quarter.
The list is very long.
items.explanation: Demande indirecte sur la localisation d'un document. (A) indique qui pourrait l'avoir.

items.audioText: Let's move the weekly briefing to Friday morning. (A) That works for me. (B) The briefing is usually very short. (C) Friday is a busy day.
choices: That works for me.
The briefing is usually very short.
Friday is a busy day.
items.explanation: Suggestion ("Let's..."). (A) marque l'accord de façon concise et appropriée.

items.audioText: I can't find the Henderson account file. (A) Henderson is one of our biggest clients. (B) It might be in the archive folder on the server. (C) I'll look into it.
choices: Henderson is one of our biggest clients.
It might be in the archive folder on the server.
I'll look into it.
items.explanation: Énoncé d'un problème (fichier introuvable). (B) propose une piste concrète de localisation.

items.audioText: Don't forget to register for the training session by tomorrow. (A) I already did it this morning. (B) The training is very helpful. (C) Tomorrow's schedule is full.
choices: I already did it this morning.
The training is very helpful.
Tomorrow's schedule is full.
items.explanation: Rappel ("Don't forget to..."). (A) confirme que c'est déjà fait.

items.audioText: Would it be possible to extend the project deadline by one week? (A) The project was approved in January. (B) I'll need to check with the client first. (C) The deadline is very tight.
choices: The project was approved in January.
I'll need to check with the client first.
The deadline is very tight.
items.explanation: Demande de prolongation. (B) répond de façon conditionnelle et professionnelle.

items.audioText: Someone left their laptop in the conference room. (A) Probably one of the people from the morning meeting. (B) The conference room is available now. (C) Laptops must be kept secure.
choices: Probably one of the people from the morning meeting.
The conference room is available now.
Laptops must be kept secure.
items.explanation: Constat d'une situation. (A) propose une explication logique sur qui a pu l'oublier.

items.audioText: The printer on the second floor is out of paper. (A) I'll bring a ream up from the supply room. (B) The printer was repaired last week. (C) We use it every day.
choices: I'll bring a ream up from the supply room.
The printer was repaired last week.
We use it every day.
items.explanation: Constat d'un problème. (A) propose immédiatement une solution.

items.audioText: I'm not sure I understand the new expense policy. (A) The policy was updated in March. (B) I can walk you through it if you like. (C) You need to keep all receipts.
choices: The policy was updated in March.
I can walk you through it if you like.
You need to keep all receipts.
items.explanation: Expression d'une incompréhension. (B) offre de l'aide de façon appropriée et directe.

items.audioText: The new intern starts on Monday. (A) Has someone set up her workstation? (B) She studied marketing. (C) Interns usually work part-time.
choices: Has someone set up her workstation?
She studied marketing.
Interns usually work part-time.
items.explanation: Annonce d'une arrivée. (A) réagit avec une question pratique et pertinente sur la préparation.

items.audioText: We've exceeded our sales target for the third month in a row. (A) Congratulations — that's great news! (B) The target was set in January. (C) Sales figures are reviewed monthly.
choices: Congratulations — that's great news!
The target was set in January.
Sales figures are reviewed monthly.
items.explanation: Annonce d'un succès. (A) répond de façon naturelle et appropriée en félicitant.
